final_answer: First, add application‑level rate limiting or login throttling per IP and per user (e.g., limit the number of login attempts in a short window, apply exponential back‑off or temporary lockout). This directly cuts the flood of failed attempts, reducing the number of DB writes and reads that cause lock contention, while also mitigating brute‑force attacks. After rate limiting is in place, address the remaining DB contention by reviewing indexes and keeping login transactions short.
